title: Association between levels of persistent organic pollutants in adipose tissue and cryptorchidism in early childhood: a case-control study.
ehr_evidence: The study used subcutaneous adipose tissue biopsies taken during surgery, not electronic health records.
summary: A case-control study investigating the association between prenatal exposure to persistent organic pollutants (PCBs, PCDD/Fs, and PBDEs) in adipose tissue and the risk of cryptorchidism in children.
key_findings: Total-TEq (dioxins and dioxin-like PCBs) and sum of PCDD/Fs were positively associated with cryptorchidism (OR 3.21 and 3.69, respectively).
Sum of PCBs was close to significant (OR 1.92).
Sum of PBDEs was not significantly associated (OR 0.86).
population: children (44 cryptorchid cases, 38 controls) in Finland and Denmark
